answer:
**Iqbal's poem "Sayed Ki Loh-e-Turbat" ("The Epitaph of the Sayed") from *Bang-E-Dara Part 1* (ch. 38) depicts the Sayed's gravestone (Loh-e-Turbat) as speaking directly, with its "carved inscriptions" conveying a series of exhortations to poets, statesmen, and believers.**[^1][^2]

The gravestone's inscription begins after evoking the scene of the soul's freedom and the revival of a once-wasted city, proclaiming:  
> See how my very tombstone is enraptured by my speech;  
> Now with your inner eye, read what these carved inscriptions teach.[^3]

**The teachings inscribed emphasize unity over division, courage in speech, rejection of hypocrisy, and the power of poetry to awaken the slumbering and ignite truth:**[^4]
- Guide people toward worldly engagement, not world-renouncing asceticism: "If your aim in this world is to teach and to guide, / Do not teach your own people to cast the world aside."[^5]
- Avoid sectarian strife: "Let not your tongue be opened for the cause of warring creeds; / A hidden tumult of the Final Day in such division breeds."[^6]
- Promote unity: "Let your writings forge the very means for unity to grow."[^7]
- Shun outdated tales and speak boldly as a statesman: "Courage is the staff of those who master statecraft's design."[^8]
- Embody fearless truth: "A true Believer's heart is free of hypocrisy and fear."[^9]
- For writers and poets: Keep speech pure and use verse to "Awaken those who slumber... / And with your voice's flame, set the harvest of all falsehood alight!"[^10][^11]

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 38 "Sayed Ki Loh-e-Turbat" (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/).
[^2]: [p-b2c140] "The Epitaph of the Sayed" (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-b2c140).
[^3]: [p-f55cb3] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-f55cb3).
[^4]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 38 (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/).
[^5]: [p-7a6aba] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-7a6aba).
[^6]: [p-72e543] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-72e543).
[^7]: [p-d3a871] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-d3a871).
[^8]: [p-ee0ccd]–[p-0cf5a4] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-ee0ccd).
[^9]: [p-c379c6] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-c379c6).
[^10]: [p-77c740]–[p-9fcf9f] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-77c740).
[^11]: [p-2e3845] (/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-2e3845).
